name: TAPS Survivor Seminars
whatItProvides: Regional and national gatherings providing grief workshops, peer groups, remembrance events, and resource panels.

name: TAPS Youth Programs
whatItProvides: Support structure, literature, and activities specifically designed for bereaved military children, siblings, and teens.

name: TAPS Good Grief Camps
whatItProvides: Camp experiences held alongside major seminars where children are paired with active duty/veteran mentors to learn healthy coping tools.

name: TAPS Young Adults Program
whatItProvides: Supports survivors aged 18-30 through life skills workshops, personal development retreats, and career mentorship.

name: TAPS Men's Program
whatItProvides: Customized retreats, peer groups, and outdoors activities designed specifically for surviving men (fathers, brothers, sons, spouses).

name: TAPS Women's Empowerment
whatItProvides: Empowerment workshops, writing retreats, and support networks designed for surviving women.

name: TAPS Peer Mentoring Program
whatItProvides: Pairs grieving survivors with trained peer mentors, and provides training for survivors (18+ months past loss) wanting to become mentors.

name: TAPS Suicide Loss Support Program
whatItProvides: Structured postvention support for survivors grieving a death by suicide, including peer connection and loss-specific workshops.

name: TAPS Sports & Entertainment (teams4taps)
whatItProvides: Creates opportunities for survivors to attend sporting events, meet athletes, and participate in memorial games.

name: TAPS Online Community
whatItProvides: Provides scheduled virtual support groups, chat rooms, and text-based forums for survivor connection.

name: TAPS Care Groups
whatItProvides: Local, volunteer-led support groups meeting monthly in libraries, community centers, and churches across the US.

name: TAPS Togethers
whatItProvides: One-time community-based outings, dinners, hikes, and volunteer projects to connect survivors locally in a relaxed environment.
